implementation_summary.overview: This document provides a summary of the proper implementation patterns for IBM App Connect Enterprise projects, with specific examples from the WeatherAPI_App project showing correct vs incorrect approaches.

weatherapi_project_corrections.description: The WeatherAPI_App project was created with proper BROKER SCHEMA declarations and follows the standards outlined in the schema_and_reference_standards guide. Here are the key implementation details:

file.name: ValidateInput.esql
file.status: Correctly implemented with proper BROKER SCHEMA

file.name: PrepareCurrentWeatherRequest.esql
file.status: Correctly implemented with EXTERNAL variable and proper schema

file.name: PrepareAlertsRequest.esql
file.status: Correctly implemented with EXTERNAL variable and proper schema

file.name: HandleError.esql
file.status: Correctly implemented with proper error handling pattern

learning.title: Generated Code Uses 'gen' Schema
learning.description: The Hoovers_ClientVerification_Service project demonstrates that generated code (from WSDL, service descriptors, etc.) should use 'gen' as the BROKER SCHEMA

learning.title: Subflow References Include Full Path
learning.description: Subflows are referenced with their full directory path, and the namespace declaration uses underscores instead of slashes

learning.title: Common Library Procedures Use Full Schema Path
learning.description: When calling procedures from shared libraries, use the full schema path

learning.title: SHARED Variables for Caching
learning.description: Use SHARED variables to maintain state across invocations, such as caching authentication tokens with timestamps

learning.title: EXTERNAL Variables for Configuration
learning.description: Use EXTERNAL variables for values that change between environments, always provide default values

learning.title: Compute Mode for LocalEnvironment Modifications
learning.description: When a Compute node modifies OutputLocalEnvironment (e.g., setting HTTP headers, request URLs, or reply status codes), the computeMode attribute MUST be set to "destinationAndMessage" in the message flow XML. This ensures ACE properly handles both the message and LocalEnvironment trees.
learning.rationale: Without the correct computeMode, modifications to OutputLocalEnvironment may not be properly propagated, leading to runtime issues such as missing HTTP headers, incorrect status codes, or failed HTTP requests.
learning.example:
-- ESQL modifying LocalEnvironment: SET OutputLocalEnvironment.Destination.HTTP.RequestURL = requestURL; SET OutputLocalEnvironment.Destination.HTTP.ReplyStatusCode = 400;
-- Corresponding message flow node MUST have computeMode attribute: <nodes xmi:type="ComIbmCompute.msgnode:FCMComposite_1" xmi:id="FCMComposite_1_2" location="138,56" computeExpression="esql://routine/com.weather.api#ValidateInput.Main" computeMode="destinationAndMessage"> <translation xmi:type="utility:ConstantString" string="Validate Input"/> </nodes>

common_scenarios.scenario: Setting HTTP request URLs for HTTP Request nodes
Setting HTTP reply status codes (200, 400, 500, etc.)
Setting HTTP headers in requests or responses

category.name: ESQL Files
category.item: ✓ Start every ESQL file with BROKER SCHEMA declaration
✓ Use consistent schema across all files in the same project
✓ Use 'gen' schema for generated code
✓ Use application-specific schema for manual code (e.g., com.weather.api)
✓ Declare EXTERNAL variables after BROKER SCHEMA, before modules
✓ Provide default values for all EXTERNAL variables
✓ Use SHARED variables for cross-invocation state (caching)
✓ Include proper module header comments with version history

category.name: Message Flows
category.item: ✓ Include all required XML namespace declarations
✓ Declare namespace for each node type used (ComIbmCompute.msgnode, etc.)
✓ Declare namespace for each subflow referenced
✓ Use correct xmi:type format: [namespace_prefix]:[NodeType]
✓ Reference ESQL with schema: esql://routine/[schema]#[Module].[Function]
✓ Reference subflows with full path: [directory]/[SubflowName].subflow
✓ Use descriptive node labels
✓ Connect error handling at flow level (Catch terminals)
✓ Set computeMode="destinationAndMessage" on Compute nodes that modify OutputLocalEnvironment

category.name: Project Structure
category.item: ✓ Create .project file with correct natures and builders
✓ Create application.descriptor with library references
✓ Create .settings/org.eclipse.core.resources.prefs with UTF-8 encoding
✓ Organize files in appropriate directories (gen/, com/org/esb/, properties/)
✓ Create environment-specific properties files (DEV, QA, UAT, PROD)
✓ Use consistent naming conventions

category.name: Deployment
category.item: ✓ Build BAR files using ibmint package command
✓ Source ACE profile before running commands
✓ Include all dependent libraries in BAR file
✓ Configure environment-specific overrides
✓ Version BAR files consistently

issue.symptom: Compute node shows error "Module not found"

possible_causes.cause: Missing BROKER SCHEMA in ESQL file
Module name doesn't match ESQL file

solution.step: Verify BROKER SCHEMA is declared at top of ESQL file

issue.symptom: Subflow node shows error "Subflow not found"

possible_causes.cause: Incorrect namespace declaration in message flow XML

solution.step: Verify xmi:type uses same namespace prefix: gen_SubflowName.subflow:FCMComposite_1
Confirm subflow file exists at specified path
Source ACE profile: source "/Applications/IBM App Connect Enterprise/server/bin/mqsiprofile"

issue.symptom: EXTERNAL variable not being overridden by properties file

possible_causes.cause: Property name doesn't match variable name
Properties file not deployed with BAR

solution.step: Ensure property name in .properties file exactly matches EXTERNAL variable name
Verify properties file is included in BAR deployment
Check correct environment properties are being applied

issue.symptom: LocalEnvironment modifications not taking effect (e.g., HTTP status codes, request URLs)

possible_causes.cause: computeMode set to wrong value (should be "destinationAndMessage")

solution.step: Identify all Compute nodes that modify OutputLocalEnvironment in their ESQL
Add computeMode="destinationAndMessage" attribute to each such Compute node in the .msgflow file
Common LocalEnvironment modifications include: HTTP.RequestURL, HTTP.ReplyStatusCode, HTTP headers, MQ properties

issue.example:
<!-- WRONG: Missing computeMode --> <nodes xmi:type="ComIbmCompute.msgnode:FCMComposite_1" computeExpression="esql://routine/com.weather.api#ValidateInput.Main">
<!-- CORRECT: With computeMode --> <nodes xmi:type="ComIbmCompute.msgnode:FCMComposite_1" computeExpression="esql://routine/com.weather.api#ValidateInput.Main" computeMode="destinationAndMessage">
